channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Dandan dumping oil down drain, 6th April
item.description:
Employees from Dandan dumping waste oil down drain in road
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 44-46, Southwark Street, The Borough, Greater London

item.title: Asbestos looking roof shingles, 6th April
item.description:
Already reported as roof tiles but these look like asbestos roofing panels. Public hazard if they are.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Stembridge Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Eden Park, Greater London

item.title: Relentless fly tipping continues, 6th April
item.description:
Household furniture and waste

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Beeston Close, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: More dumping associated with old morning lane Nike building, 6th April
item.description:
More rubbish left outside the back door of the old Nike building on morning lane. This time they haven't even bothered to move it next to the bins! This has been going on for weeks now

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Morning Lane, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Mattresses and other furniture flytipped, 6th April
item.description:
More flytipping by the recycling bin outside the dry cleaners, road that approaches Penge east station. Mattresses etc.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 210, Venner Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Advertisement on 52 Anerley hill, 6th April
item.description:
Advertisement attached to the side of 52 Anerley Hill covering and blocking light to windows that are in use by tenants of the building. This advertisement went up on Saturday 4th or Sunday 5th of April 2026. There was a 'consent to display an advertisement' application made in 2005 which is now outdated. 05/01080/ADV. This advertisement could also be in breach of Policy 102 in the Bromley Local Plan, for example A - have regard to the character of the surrounding area C - generally not be located in residential areas and the Green Belt, Metropolitan Open Land (MOL) and Urban Open Space D - preserve or enhance the character or appearance of conservation area,

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 52, Anerley Hill, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large graffiti tag on wall, 6th April
item.description:
Please cover up. thanks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Peckham Park Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large holes in football pitch, 6th April
item.description:
Some ground works we done a month or two ago and the football pitch has been left in an extremely poor (unusable and unsafe condition). The surface is very uneven, there are holes over 12 inches deep, particularly in front of the goal posts, no care has been taken to reinstate the football pitch to its former condition following the works. It’s an immediate and serious health and safety hazard as the facility is in constant use by children in the community and it’s only a matter of time before one of them breaks an ankle. The pitch often suffers from flooding so it would be ideal to landscape it with a slight camber to aid drainage, before relaying the surface. Thank you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Florence Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on wall, 6th April
item.description:
Please remove

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Peckham Park Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bun overflow and fly tipping, 6th April
item.description:
Fly tips regularly occur here - time to put in CCTV.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bloomfield Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Mattress and other rubbish dumped on the street, 6th April
item.description:
There is a mattress dumped half on the pavement and half on the street in William Booth Road roughly opposite no. 49. There are also carpet tiles and laughing gas canisters dumped on the pavement.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 33, William Booth Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Illegally parking in Vanbrugh park riad, SE37QF, 6th April
item.description:
2 vehicles have parked illegally on private land f

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Vanbrugh Fields, Blackheath

item.title: Street litter picking needed, 6th April
item.description:
Street litter picking needed

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 368, Lordship Lane, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped rubbish on the side of the path, 6th April
item.description:
There is dumped rubbish on the small path that connects Owen Walk with Anerley Road: dumped paint, laughing gas canisters and other rubbish

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rowan Terrace, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned dangerously parked vehicles, 6th April
item.description:
Over a week now that these two vehicles have been here on the double yellow line. One vehicle is untaxed and without MOT. It is parked dangerously causing a risk to both drivers and pedestrians as it is blocking your view of oncoming traffic. Lewisham parking control NSL claim the area is patrolled daily but the parking warden can’t see these two vehicles apparently. Did I mention council tax just went up!!!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rollins Street, New Cross Gate, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing litter bins, 6th April
item.description:
The litter bins at the Thicket Road entrance to the Park are constantly overflowing at the weekends. There is a need to either increase the frequency of the bins to be emptied or having additional bins there on the weekends.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Thicket Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Barrier left behind, 6th April
item.description:
A safety barrier has been left behind after drain was repaired 3 weeks ago. On pavement between 17 and 19 The Chase
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): The Chase, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Utility Works and Temporary Traffic Lights

item.title: Double mattress dumped outside 54/56 Ormiston Rd, 6th April
item.description:
This afternoon a double mattress has been flytipped outside 54/56 Ormiston Road

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Station Crescent, Blackheath
